description: Substantive and procedural aspects of criminal law, including the purposes of criminal justice, specific crimes, criminal responsibility and punishment, legal concepts of proof, and 4th, 5th, and 6th Amendment issues. The case approach is used. Not open to students with credit for POLI SCI 452 prior to fall 2017

optimized_prerequisites.prerequisites_text: Sophomore standing and a course in POLI SCI or SOC

prerequisites.prerequisites_text: Sophomore standing and a course in POLI SCI or SOC
